name: GERD - Comprehensive Provider Query
description: Simulates a primary care provider identifying their complete GERD-treated patient population. The PheKB GERD algorithm itself uses 2+ dx + 2+ meds (or 3+ dx alone). Real-world identification primarily unions: GERD diagnosis codes and PPI prescriptions. Because PPI prescribing is the most cross-indication-heavy of any drug class, the union of dx OR PPI overcounts substantially — but it captures the full population at risk for chronic acid suppression complications.
prompt:
I need to identify my complete GERD-treated patient population for a quality initiative on chronic PPI use and deprescribing. From clinical experience, PPIs are widely prescribed: most patients with a formal GERD diagnosis are on a PPI, but many PPI patients don't have a formal reflux diagnosis (started empirically for dyspepsia, NSAID prophylaxis, or other indications).

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any GERD-related diagnosis (GERD, acid reflux, heartburn)
2. Patients prescribed any proton pump inhibitor

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found. The medication query will substantially overcount because of PPI cross-indication prescribing, but that overcount itself is clinically important — chronic PPI exposure has the same risks regardless of indication.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Union of GERD dx codes (Paths A+B) and PPI Rx (Paths A+C). Path C is dominant (~50%) reflecting real-world PPI cross-indication prescribing. The PheKB algorithm intersects (dx AND meds), but providers screen with the union to find the population at risk for chronic PPI complications.
